summary: Clarify timesheet approval chain rendering by separating active approval nodes from cancelled historical route nodes.

notes: Approval chain data continues to come from psa_timesheet_approval_chain.
Expanded approval chains now show the current effective route separately from historical cancelled route nodes.
Cancelled nodes are labeled as historical and marked as not participating in current approval.
Project-scoped approval nodes display project code and project name when available.
Rejected nodes explicitly show their rollback path to node 1 pending submission.

deployment.cloud: Build frontend with VITE_APP_VERSION=V0.17.2 and production VITE variables, sync frontend/dist, rebuild the app image with APP_IMAGE_TAG=V0.17.2, then run pre-deploy and frontend render smoke checks.
notes: Server app directory is overlaid from the GitHub tag archive.
frontend/dist is built locally because the server does not have npm.

verification: Run deploy/scripts/pre-deploy-check.sh on the cloud server with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.17.2.
known_warnings: ESLint still reports existing warnings in dashboard/ui/TimesheetPage files, but exits 0.
Vite still warns that the main index chunk is larger than 500 kB.
